messages.content: You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
Kya paddy ke khete mein mathia ko khatam karne ki koi aasani wali aam tarika hai?

Haan, mathia ko khatam karne ke liye aap pre-emergence herbicides ka istemal karein. 3 din baad transplant karne ke baad Pretilachlor 50% EC 600 ml/acre ya Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP 60 gm/acre ka istemal karein. Agar mathia daud raha hai to Bispyribac sodium 10% SC 100 ml/acre 20-25 din baad istemal karein. Hath se khatme bhi karna hota hai 20-25 aur 40-45 din baad. Herbiside ka istemal karne time 2-3 inch pani rakhna hota hai.
Herbiside ka kis tarah istemal karna hota hai?
Herbiside ka istemal karne time 2-3 inch pani rakhna hota hai.
Pretilachlor 50% EC ka kitna dosha istemal karna hota hai?
Pretilachlor 50% EC ka istemal 600 ml/acre karne ka sara hai.
Kab tak herbiside ka istemal karna chahiye?
3 din baad transplant karne ke baad Pretilachlor 50% EC ya Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP ka istemal karein. Agar mathia daud raha hai to Bispyribac sodium 10% SC 20-25 din baad istemal karein.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
Is it okay to use Nirog on my wheat seeds to keep them healthy?

Yes, you can use Nirog on your wheat seeds. It's a bio-fungicide with Trichoderma viride. For loose smut, mix 4 grams of it per kg of seeds. Make sure the seeds are coated well and dry them in the shade before sowing. Always check the label to make sure it's registered and buy from a trusted dealer.
When should Nirog be applied during the wheat crop cycle?
Nirog should be applied before sowing by mixing 4 grams per kg of seeds, coating them well, and drying in the shade prior to sowing.
How much Nirog should I use per kg of seeds?
For loose smut, mix 4 grams of it per kg of seeds.
How to apply Nirog on wheat seeds?
Mix 4 grams of Nirog per kg of seeds, coat the seeds well, and dry them in the shade before sowing.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My wheat plants are getting weak and dying, what could be the problem?

It might be termites. They attack the roots and make plants easy to pull out. Yellow leaves and stunted growth are signs. To manage them, treat seeds with Chlorpyriphos or thiomethoxam. Use Beauveria bassiana in the soil. Keep fields clean and avoid too much organic matter. Apply Chlorpyrifos with water if needed. Stay alert, especially in October and February.
How to apply Chlorpyrifos?
Apply Chlorpyrifos with water if needed.
Kab tak Chlorpyrifos apply karna chahiye?
October aur February mein chinta rakhna chahiye.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
I heard about a product called Nirog for treating my wheat seeds. Is it safe to use?

Yes, you can use Nirog for treating wheat seeds, but it's a bio-fungicide with Trichoderma viride 1.5% WP. For loose smut, use 4 grams per kg of seeds. Mix it well, shade dry, and then sow. Always check the label for active ingredients and make sure it's registered. Buy from authorized dealers and check the batch number.
Nirog kya kharap kharab seed ke liye effective hai?

Yes, you can use Nirog for treating wheat seeds, but it's a bio-fungicide with Trichoderma viride 1.5% WP. For loose smut, use 4 grams per kg of seeds. Mix it well, shade dry, and then sow. Always check the label for active ingredients and make sure it's registered. Buy from authorized dealers and check the batch number.
Kya Nirog ko kis waqt lagana chahiye?
Nirog को बीज के साथ मिलाकर छाया में सुखाकर बोएं। इसे बोने से पहले लागू करें और लेबल पर दिए गए निर्देशों का पालन करें।
Nirog me kya active ingredients hain?
Nirog ek bio-fungicide hai jisme Trichoderma viride 1.5% WP hota hai.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
Can I use Nirog to treat my wheat seeds?

Yes, you can treat wheat seeds with Nirog. It's a bio-fungicide with Trichoderma viride. For loose smut, use 4 grams per kg of seeds. Mix well, dry in shade, and sow. Also, check the label to make sure it's registered. Buy from authorized dealers and use the right dose.
What precautions should I take while using Nirog?
Check the label to make sure it's registered. Buy from authorized dealers and use the right dose.
What are the specific ingredients in Nirog?
Nirog is a bio-fungicide containing Trichoderma viride.
Is Nirog registered?
Check the label to make sure it's registered.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My paddy field has a lot of wild onion, what can I do about it?

You can control wild onion in your paddy by using herbicides. Apply Pretilachlor 50% EC at 600 ml per acre or Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP at 60 gm per acre within 3 days after transplanting. For later control, use Bispyribac sodium 10% SC at 100 ml per acre at 20-25 days after transplanting. Also, hand weeding at 20-25 and 40-45 days after transplanting works well. Keep 2-3 inches of water in the field when applying the chemicals.
How many times should I apply the herbicides?

You should apply Pretilachlor 50% EC or Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP once within 3 days after transplanting, and Bispyribac sodium 10% SC once at 20-25 days after transplanting. Hand weeding is done twice, at 20-25 and 40-45 days after transplanting.
How much water should be kept in the field while applying the herbicides?
Keep 2-3 inches of water in the field when applying the chemicals.
What are the exact timings for each herbicide application?
Apply Pretilachlor 50% EC or Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP within 3 days after transplanting, and Bispyribac sodium 10% SC at 20-25 days after transplanting.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
Is Nirog a good option to use on my wheat seeds?

Nirog is a bio-fungicide that contains Trichoderma viride 1.5% WP. It can be used for treating wheat seeds, but you need to make sure it's registered by the Central Insecticides Board. Always check the label for the active ingredients and follow the recommended dosage. If you're not sure, ask your local dealer for help.
Kis tarah Nirog ka prayog wheat ke bhijan par karna chahiye?

Nirog ek biyajan itna hai jo Trichoderma viride 1.5% WP ko contain karta hai. Aap wheat ke bhijan ke liye iska prayog kar sakte hain, lekin aapko Central Insecticides Board dvara uska pramān hone ka dhyan rakhna chahiye. Label par aktif anupāt ko check karein aur prasiddh dosha ko follow karein. Agar aapke paas shak hai, to aap apne koi local dealer se madad lena chahiye.
Kitni dosha ka prayog karna chahiye?
Nirog ka prayog karte samay label par likhe aktif anupaat ko dhyan se dekhein aur prasiddh dosha ko follow karein. Agar aapke paas shak hai, to aap apne koi local dealer se madad lena chahiye.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My wheat plants are looking weak and some are drying up, what's going on?

It sounds like termites might be the problem. They attack the roots and lower stems of wheat plants, causing yellowing, stunted growth, and plants that pull out easily. You can try treating the seeds before sowing with Chlorpyriphos 20% EC or thiomethoxam 30% FS. Also, make sure to keep the field clean and use well-decomposed manure. If the problem is already there, you can use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC with irrigation water. Be careful when using chemicals and follow the instructions closely.
Kab tak Chlorpyrifos 20% EC ka istemal karna chaheye?
Chlorpyrifos 20% EC ka istemal pahle seed chawal ke sowing ke samay ya jab sas ke problem pahle se hai to irrigation ke sath karna chahiye.
Chlorpyrifos 20% EC ka kya istemal karna chahiye?
Chlorpyrifos 20% EC ko irrigation ke sath istemal karna chahiye.
Chlorpyrifos 20% EC ka kya dosha hota hai?
Chlorpyrifos 20% EC ka dosha product ke instructions ke anusaar hona chahiye.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My wheat plants are not growing well, and some are getting pulled out easily. What could be the problem?

It might be termite damage. Termites attack the roots and lower stem of wheat plants, causing them to dry up and be easily pulled out. This happens more in fields with lots of organic matter or near trees. To manage it, you can use chlorpyrifos or thiomethoxam for seed treatment, apply biopesticides like Beauveria bassiana, and keep the field clean by removing stubble and debris. Also, make sure to irrigate properly and avoid using fresh organic matter.
What is the correct dosage for chlorpyrifos or thiomethoxam when treating wheat seeds?

The exact dosage for chlorpyrifos or thiomethoxam should follow the manufacturer's guidelines on the product label. Always adhere to recommended rates to ensure effective control without harming the crop.
How should I apply chlorpyrifos or thiomethoxam to the wheat seeds?

Chlorpyrifos or thiomethoxam should be used for seed treatment as per the manufacturer's guidelines on the product label. Always adhere to recommended rates to ensure effective control without harming the crop.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My paddy fields are getting damaged by some bugs. What can I do?

The bugs are called Gundhi Bugs. They suck sap from the rice grains, making them shriveled and lighter. You can use insecticides like Malathion or Imidacloprid. Apply them in the early morning or late evening. You can also use neem extract. Wear gloves and a mask when spraying. Keep your fields clean and remove weeds. Don't spray during flowering or when it's windy.
How should I apply the insecticides?
You can spray them in the early morning or late evening.
What preventive measures can I take?
Keep your fields clean and remove weeds.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My paddy field has wild onions, how do I get rid of them?

You can control wild onions in paddy by using herbicides. Apply Pretilachlor 50% EC at 600 ml per acre or Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP at 60 grams per acre within 3 days of transplanting. For later stages, use Bispyribac sodium 10% SC at 100 ml per acre at 20-25 days after transplanting. Also, hand weed the field at 20-25 and 40-45 days. Keep 2-3 inches of water in the field when applying chemicals.
How many times should I apply the herbicides?

Apply Pretilachlor 50% EC or Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP once within 3 days of transplanting, and Bispyribac sodium 10% SC once at 20-25 days after transplanting. Hand weed the field at 20-25 and 40-45 days.
Kab tak paani rakhna chahiye chemicals lagane ke baad?
Chemicals lagane ke samay khepta me 2-3 inch paani rakhna chahiye.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
Is Nirog a good option for treating my wheat seeds?

Yes, Nirog is a biological fungicide that contains Trichoderma viride 1.5% WP. It helps protect wheat seeds from diseases like loose smut and seedling blights. But make sure to check the label for active ingredients and follow the dosage instructions. Always buy genuine products from authorized dealers and check the manufacturing date.
How often should I apply Nirog to my wheat seeds?
Follow the dosage instructions mentioned on the label for application frequency.
What is the recommended dosage for Nirog?
Follow the dosage instructions mentioned on the label.
How to apply Nirog to wheat seeds?
Follow the dosage instructions mentioned on the label.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My paddy fields are getting attacked by some bugs that make the grains look bad, what can I do?

The bugs you're seeing are called Gundhi Bugs. They feed on rice grains and make them shriveled and discolored. You can use insecticides like Malathion, Imidacloprid, or Dinotefuran. Apply them in the early morning or evening. Also, try using neem seed extract. Keep your field clean and remove weeds. Wear gloves and a mask when spraying. Don't spray during wind or when flowers are open.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid this issue?
Keep your field clean and remove weeds.
What safety measures should I take while spraying insecticides?
Wear gloves and a mask when spraying. Don't spray during wind or when flowers are open.
How should I apply the insecticides?
Apply them in the early morning or evening. Wear gloves and a mask when spraying.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My paddy plants are getting damaged, and the grains are not filling properly. What could be the problem?

It sounds like you might have Gundhi Bug, also called Naavaai Poochi. These bugs are greenish-brown and can be found on the rice plants. They suck the sap from the grains, making them shriveled and discolored. This happens mostly during the milky stage of the crop. If you see a lot of these bugs, you might need to spray insecticides like Malathion or Imidacloprid. You can also try using neem extract. Make sure to wear protective gear when applying chemicals. Always follow the dosage instructions carefully.
Kitna dosh lagana chahiye Malathion ya Imidacloprid ke?

Dosage instructions should be followed carefully as per the product label. Always check the recommended concentration and volume for spraying, and avoid exceeding the specified amount to prevent crop damage or chemical waste.
Gundhi Bug ke liye insecticide kis waqt spray karna chahiye?
Gundhi Bug ke liye insecticide spray milky stage mein karna chahiye, jab seed shriveled aur discolored hote hain.
Insecticide spray karte waqt kya safety measures rakhi jaye?
Protective gear ka upyog karein aur dosage instructions ko dhyan se follow karein.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
I've noticed some tiny bugs on my wheat plants, what should I do?

Those tiny bugs might be thrips, not whiteflies. They're small, brown or black, and move fast. They feed on the stem and sheath of the flag leaf, and can make the plant look silver. To check, use blue pan traps or sticky traps. You can paint tins blue or use Vaseline on them. Check the traps daily. If you see insects, take a sample to the nearest KVK for help identifying the pest and getting the right control advice.
Kya mein blue pan traps ka istemal kaise kare?

Blue pan traps ya sticky traps ka istemal karte samay, tins ko blue rakho ya Vaseline lagaye. Inhe field mein rakhke har din dekhein. Agar aapko insects dekhein, toh nearest KVK par sample leke jayein.
Kya thrips ko nahi nikaalne par kya hota hai?

Agar thrips ko nahi nikaala jaye, toh wo phale ke stemb aur sheath ko kha sakte hain, jiske karan plant silver dikhne lagta hai aur pranaliya kshatit ho sakti hai. Iske liye aapko trap lagaye rakhna chahiye aur agari insects dikhe toh nearest KVK par sample leke jayein.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
Kya meri chawal ki seedhi mein kuch nahi karna chahiye agar kuch khurang jaise chiz nikal rahi hai?

Agar chawal ki seedhi mein khurang jaise chiz nikal rahi hai, toh usko control karne ke liye pre-emergence herbicides jaise Pretilachlor 50% EC ya Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP ka upyog karein. 3 din ke andar paribandhi karte samay iska upyog karein. Agar khurang badh gaya, toh Bispyribac sodium 10% SC ka upyog 20-25 din baad karein. Hathi se khurang hatane ka bhi upyog karein. Har baar 2-3 inch pani rakhna chahiye.
Kitne baar yeh herbicides apply karna chahiye?
Pehle 3 din ke andar paribandhi karte samay ek baar aur agar khurang badh gaya toh 20-25 din baad doosra baar.
Pani ka kya level rakhna chahiye?
Har baar 2-3 inch pani rakhna chahiye.
Kaise apply karna chahiye?
Paribandhi karte samay iska upyog karein.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
I'm seeing some small bugs on my wheat plants, what could they be and how do I get rid of them?

Those bugs might be thrips, not whiteflies. They're tiny, brown or black, and move fast. They damage the stem and the sheath of the flag leaf, and can make the plant look silver. To check, use blue sticky traps or paint a tin blue and put it above the crop. Watch the traps daily. If you see bugs, bring a sample to the nearest KVK for help. Don't use chemicals unless you know exactly what you're dealing with.
What safety precautions should I take when using chemicals for thrips?
Avoid using chemicals unless you are certain of the pest and have guidance from KVK to ensure safety.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
I noticed small brown bugs on my wheat plants, what are they and how do I deal with them?

Those small brown bugs are thrips. They can damage the wheat by feeding on the stem and leaves, causing a silver color on the plant. To check for thrips, use blue pan traps or sticky traps. You can paint empty tins blue or use grease on them. Count the bugs daily and decide on the next step. If you're not sure, take a sample to the nearest KVK for help.
How do I make blue pan traps for thrips?
You can paint empty tins blue or use grease on them.
How often should I check the traps?
Count the bugs daily and decide on the next step.
What should I do if I'm not sure about the thrips?
If you're not sure, take a sample to the nearest KVK for help.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
The leaves of my berseem crop are getting eaten up, what's causing this and how can I fix it?

The damage to your berseem crop is likely caused by the Bihar hairy caterpillar or gram caterpillar. These pests attack the crop in two phases, one in September-October and again in March-April. To prevent this, remove weeds like Gutpatna, Bathu, Jangli Palak, and wild castor near your fields before sowing. Also, avoid growing berseem near tomato, gram, or other crops that attract the pest. If needed, spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62ml per acre when the pest is at an economic threshold level.
When is the best time to spray Spinosad for Bihar hairy caterpillar?

Spray Spinosad 45% SC during the active periods of the Bihar hairy caterpillar, which are September-October and March-April, but only when the pest reaches an economic threshold level. This ensures effective control without unnecessary pesticide use.
What is the correct dosage for Spinosad 45% SC when spraying for Bihar hairy caterpillar?
Spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62ml per acre when the pest is at an economic threshold level.
How should I apply Spinosad 45% SC for Bihar hairy caterpillar?
Spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62ml per acre when the pest is at an economic threshold level.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
How do I stop termites from eating my wheat plants?

Termites can damage wheat plants by eating their roots. To stop them, treat the seeds with Chlorpyriphos 20% EC or thiomethoxam 30% FS before sowing. You can also mix Beauvaria bassiana 1.15% with farmyard manure and apply it to the soil. If termites are already in the field, use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC with irrigation water. Keep the field clean and avoid leaving crop residues. Also, make sure the soil isn't too dry.
How to apply Beauvaria bassiana 1.15%?
Mix Beauvaria bassiana 1.15% with farmyard manure and apply it to the soil.
How much Beauvaria bassiana 1.15% should I mix with farmyard manure?
The quantity of Beauvaria bassiana 1.15% to be mixed with farmyard manure is not specified in the provided information. Please refer to the product's guidelines for the correct dosage.
When should I apply Beauvaria bassiana 1.15% in the wheat crop cycle?
The timing for applying Beauvaria bassiana 1.15% is not specified in the provided information. Please refer to the product's guidelines for the appropriate stage of the wheat crop.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My stored wheat is getting attacked by little red bugs, what can I do?

Those red bugs are called rust-red flour beetles. They damage stored wheat by eating it and leaving a musty smell. To stop them, keep your storage area clean and dry. Dry the wheat properly before storing and use sealed containers. For small amounts, expose the wheat to sunlight for a few hours. If it's badly infested, you can use fumigation with Aluminum Phosphide, but it needs to be done carefully. Always follow safety steps and make sure the area is well-ventilated before using the grain again.
What safety steps should I take while using Aluminum Phosphide?
Always follow safety steps and make sure the area is well-ventilated before using the grain again.
How much Aluminum Phosphide should I use for fumigation?

The exact dosage depends on the infestation level and storage size. Always follow manufacturer guidelines and safety protocols when applying. For precise measurements, consult the product label or local agricultural experts.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
What can I do to get rid of flour beetles in my stored wheat?

To get rid of flour beetles in stored wheat, you can expose the grain to direct sunlight for 4-5 hours, which kills all stages of the beetle. For small amounts, sieve the grain to remove beetles and larvae. Store wheat in airtight containers or sealed metallic bins. If the infestation is heavy, use Aluminum Phosphide 56% for fumigation. Follow the recommended dosage and make sure the storage area is completely sealed. After fumigation, aerate the area for 48-72 hours before handling the grain. You can also mix diatomaceous earth or neem seed kernel powder with the grain for added protection.
What are the specific chemical ingredients used in the treatments?
The specific chemical ingredients used are Aluminum Phosphide 56%, diatomaceous earth, and neem seed kernel powder.
What is the recommended dosage for Aluminum Phosphide 56% when fumigating wheat?
The recommended dosage for Aluminum Phosphide 56% should be followed as per the manufacturer's instructions, as the exact quantity is not specified in the provided information.
How to apply Aluminum Phosphide 56% for fumigation?

Apply Aluminum Phosphide 56% as per the manufacturer's instructions, ensuring the storage area is completely sealed during fumigation. Afterward, aerate the area for 48-72 hours before handling the grain.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My sugarcane plants are getting damaged by borers, what can I do?

Borers can really hurt your sugarcane crop. There are different types, like the early shoot borer and the internode borer. They cause things like dead hearts and weak stems. To control them, you can use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC at 2.5 liters per hectare as a soil drench. You can also use Carbofuran 3% CG or Phorate 10% CG. Always wear protection when handling these chemicals. You can also use natural enemies like Trichogramma chilonis and Cotesia flavipes. Keep an eye on your plants and act when you see 5-10% of them showing signs of damage.
How should I apply Chlorpyrifos 20% EC?
Apply Chlorpyrifos 20% EC at 2.5 liters per hectare as a soil drench.
What safety precautions should I take while using Chlorpyrifos?
Always wear protection when handling these chemicals.
When should I apply the chemicals for borer control?
Apply when you see 5-10% of your plants showing signs of damage.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
What can I do to stop the caterpillars from eating my berseem plants?

To stop the caterpillars from damaging your berseem plants, you need to get rid of weeds like Gutpatna, Bathu, Jangli Palak, and wild castor near your fields before planting. These weeds are where the pests live and then move to your berseem. Also, keep your berseem away from crops like tomato, gram, and sunflower. If needed, spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62ml per acre when the pests are a problem.
How should I apply Spinosad 45% SC for caterpillars in berseem?
Spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62ml per acre when the pests are a problem.
When is the best time to apply Spinosad 45% SC for caterpillars in berseem?
Spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62ml per acre when the pests are a problem.
What is the dosage of Spinosad 45% SC for caterpillars in berseem?
Spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62ml per acre when the pests are a problem.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My sugarcane plants are getting damaged, and I think it's because of borers. What can I do?

Borers can really hurt your sugarcane. They cause dead hearts, bunchy tops, and tunnels inside the stems. To control early shoot borer, you can use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC. Apply 2.5 liters per hectare as a soil drench. You can also use Carbofuran 3% CG or Phorate 10% CG. Wear protective gear when handling chemicals. Release Trichogramma chilonis wasps and Cotesia flavipes to help control the pests. Remove dead hearts and infested parts. Check your fields regularly and act when 5-10% of plants show symptoms.
When should I apply the chemicals for best results?
Check your fields regularly and act when 5-10% of plants show symptoms.
What is the correct dosage for Chlorpyrifos 20% EC?
Apply 2.5 liters per hectare as a soil drench.
What is the correct method to apply Chlorpyrifos 20% EC?
Apply 2.5 liters per hectare as a soil drench.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My wheat plants are getting some kind of bug that's making the leaves look weird, what could it be?

It might not be whitefly. The problem could be thrips. They are small, brown or black insects that feed on the stem and sheath of the flag leaf. They can make the plant look silver. You can use blue pan traps to check for them. You can paint empty tins blue or use grease, Vaseline, or castor oil on them. Check the traps daily. If you see insects, bring a sample to the nearest KVK for help.
What are the specific chemicals or substances I can use on the traps?
You can use grease, Vaseline, or castor oil on the traps.
How do I apply the grease or Vaseline on the traps?
You can paint empty tins blue and apply grease, Vaseline, or castor oil on them to make the traps effective.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
How can I tell if my paddy has Gundhi Bug, and what can I do if it's there?

If your paddy has Gundhi Bug, you might see yellowish or shriveled grains, and the bugs might smell bad when you touch them. They are greenish-brown and about 15-18 mm long. If you find more than 5 bugs per 100 ear heads during flowering or 16 bugs after the milky stage, it's time to act. You can use insecticides like Malathion or Imidacloprid, or try neem extract. Always wear gloves and a mask when spraying. Don't spray during wind or when flowers are open. Keep pesticides in safe places and wash after use.
When should I apply the insecticides for Gundhi Bug?
You should apply insecticides during flowering if you find more than 5 bugs per 100 ear heads, or after the milky stage if there are more than 16 bugs.
What are the alternative methods to insecticides for controlling Gundhi Bug?
You can try neem extract as an alternative.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My berseem plants are getting eaten up, what's causing it and how can I stop it?

The damage to your berseem plants is likely caused by Bihar hairy caterpillar or gram caterpillar. These pests attack the crop at different times of the year. To prevent this, remove weeds like Gutpatna, Bathu, Jangli Palak, and wild castor near your fields before sowing. Also, avoid growing berseem near tomato, gram, or sunflower. If needed, spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62 ml per acre when the pest is present.
When should I apply Spinosad 45% SC for best results?
Spray Spinosad 45% SC at 60-62 ml per acre when the pest is present.
Kya Spinosad 45% SC ke sath koi aur chemical ko mix karna chahiye?
Spinosad 45% SC ko kisi bhi dusre rashi ke sath mix karne ki jaroorat nahi hai. Jab bhi kīḍa dikhaye, 60-62 ml per acre ke rate se spray karein.
Kitne baar Spinosad 45% SC apply karna chahiye?
Jab bhi kīḍa dikhaye, 60-62 ml per acre ke rate se spray karein.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
Why are my sugarcane plants not producing enough shoots this season?

Your sugarcane might not be producing enough shoots because of short days, not enough water during the tillering stage, or a lack of nutrients like phosphorus, copper, or zinc. Poor irrigation, low phosphorus, or copper deficiency can also cause yellowing or discoloration of leaves and weak growth. Zinc deficiency can lead to yellow leaf blades and short stalks. To help, make sure you water enough during key growth stages, use 2% DAP foliar spray twice, and apply the right fertilizers. Test your soil before planting and use certified seeds to avoid diseases.
How much DAP should I use for the foliar spray?
Use 2% DAP foliar spray twice.
When should I apply the DAP foliar spray?
Apply the 2% DAP foliar spray during key growth stages, such as tillering, to ensure proper development.
How to manage irrigation for sugarcane?
Make sure you water enough during key growth stages, such as tillering, to support proper development.
You are a helpful and knowledgeable agricultural advisory assistant.
My wheat plants are getting weak and dying, what's causing it?

Your wheat plants might be getting attacked by termites, which are also called deemak or geda. These pests live underground and damage the roots and stems of the plants. You might see yellowing leaves, stunted growth, and plants that can be pulled out easily. To manage them, you can use pesticides like Chlorpyrifos 20% EC or treat seeds with thiomethoxam. You can also use biopesticides like Beauvaria bassiana. Keep your field clean and avoid leaving crop residues. Make sure to follow safety rules when using chemicals.

My pea plants' lower leaves are turning yellow and the stems feel weak, what can I do?

My paddy seedlings got hit by hail, and now they look all torn up. What can I do?

After a hailstorm, check your paddy seedlings for torn leaves, broken stems, or bruised areas. These injuries can let in fungus or bacteria. Drain any water from the nursery beds right away and remove the worst damaged plants. Gently straighten the ones that are bent. You can also spray a fungicide like Carbendazim or Mancozeb to protect the seedlings from infection. If the problem is bad, you might need to start over with new seedlings.

Why are the bottom leaves on my pea plants turning yellow and what can I do about it?

The yellowing of lower leaves in pea plants is a sign of wilt disease. This happens due to root rot caused by fungi like Fusarium oxysporum and Rhizoctonia solani. The xylem in the plant turns brown and gets distorted. To manage this, treat the seeds with Carbendazim 50% WP 1 g or Captan 2 g per kg of seed. Also, use 8 tonnes of farmyard manure and apply 20 kg of N and 25 kg P2O5 per acre. You can also use Rhizobiumleguminosarum culture to help the plants grow better.

Wheat crop is getting attacked by chepa, what can I do?

Aphids, called chepa, are a big problem for wheat. They suck the sap, make leaves curl and turn yellow, and can cause big yield loss. To control them, use seeds treated with Imidacloprid. You can also spray Malathion or Thiamethoxam when there are 10-15 aphids per earhead. Spray in the morning or evening, and cover both sides of the leaves. In Punjab, do this in April or May after wheat harvest. Water the field 2–3 times to help break down the organic matter. Also, plow deep to improve drainage and use dhaincha as green manure. If the soil has a lot of sodium, use gypsum based on soil test results. Keep adding organic matter every season and choose salt-tolerant paddy varieties.
How deep should I mix the organic matter into the soil?
Mix it into the soil up to 15–20 cm deep.
How often should I add organic matter to the soil?
Keep adding organic matter every season.

Water the field 2–3 times to help break down the organic matter.

My wheat crop is getting attacked by small green bugs, what can I do?

These small green bugs are aphids, also called 'chepa'. They suck the sap from the wheat plants, causing yellowing and curling of the leaves. To control them, you can use Imidacloprid as a seed treatment. For spraying, you can use Malathion 50% EC or Thiamethoxam 25% WG. Apply the spray when there are 10-15 aphids per earhead. Spray in the early morning or late evening. Repeat the spray after 10-15 days if needed.

My paddy field has salty soil, what can I do to fix it?

To fix salty soil in your paddy field, add organic matter like paddy or wheat straw. Mix it into the soil up to 15–20 cm deep in April or May. I think I heard about a fertilizer called 20:20:13 for my maize. Is that right, and can I spray it on the leaves?

The fertilizer 20:20:13 doesn't exist. You might be thinking of 20:20:0:13, which gives nitrogen, phosphorus, and sulfur but no potassium. This fertilizer is meant for soil application, not spraying on leaves. You should apply it during sowing by broadcasting. Don't mix it with standing crops. If your crop shows sulfur deficiency, you can use magnesium sulfate at 1% (10 grams in 1 liter water). Always do a soil test before applying any fertilizer.
Can I apply this fertilizer during the growing season?
No, it should be applied during sowing by broadcasting and not mixed with standing crops.
Are there any safety precautions when using this fertilizer?

It sounds like you might have aphids, also called 'chepa.' These tiny insects suck the sap from your wheat plants, causing yellowing and curling of leaves. They also leave a sticky coating that can lead to mold. To control them, you can use insecticides like Malathion or Thiamethoxam. What's the right amount of fertilizer to use when planting sugarcane?

At planting time, sugarcane needs a good mix of nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium. A general recommendation is 60 kg of nitrogen, which is about 130 kg of urea, for planting. If the soil is low in phosphorus and potassium, add 12 kg of phosphorus and 30 kg of potassium per acre. Apply all the phosphorus and potassium right at planting. For ratoon crops, give one-third of the nitrogen in February. If the soil is dry, apply half the nitrogen at planting and the rest when it rains. Fertilizers should go in the furrow, mixed with the soil, and kept 5-7 cm away from the sugarcane sets to avoid burning. You can also add 8 tonnes of farmyard manure per acre before planting.

If the soil is dry, apply half the nitrogen at planting and the rest when it rains.

What fertilizers should I use for my mustard crop and when?

For your mustard crop, apply 18 kg urea, 101 kg SSP, and 27 kg MOP per acre at sowing as a basal dose. SSP gives phosphorus for strong roots, while urea and MOP provide nitrogen and potash. Later, apply 35 kg urea at the branching stage and 18 kg urea at the flowering stage. Also, water the crop twice during these stages. If your soil is low in sulfur, add 18 kg per acre.
How many times should I water the mustard crop during the growth stages?
Water the crop twice during these stages.
What if my soil is low in sulfur?
If your soil is low in sulfur, add 18 kg per acre.

My wheat plants are falling over, will that affect how much grain I get?

Yes, if your wheat plants are falling over, it can definitely hurt your yield. When the plants bend or fall, they get less sunlight and don't make as much food. This makes the grains not fill properly. In India, lodging can cut wheat yields by 12 to 66 percent. If it happens when the grains are forming, the loss can be as high as 80 percent. It also makes harvesting harder and can lead to more disease.
At which stage of wheat growth is lodging most harmful to the crop?
Lodging is most harmful when it happens during the grain formation stage, as this can cause yield losses as high as 80 percent.
What are the preventive measures to avoid lodging in wheat?

To prevent lodging in wheat, focus on proper management practices such as avoiding excessive nitrogen fertilization, maintaining optimal planting density, and using resistant wheat varieties. Proper irrigation and timely harvesting can also reduce lodging risk.
What happens if lodging is left untreated in wheat crops?

If lodging is left untreated, it can lead to significant yield losses (12-66% in India, up to 80% during grain formation), make harvesting harder, and increase the risk of disease due to reduced sunlight and improper grain filling.

What should I do if my sugarcane is showing yellow leaves in August?

If your sugarcane has yellow leaves in August, it might be due to iron deficiency. This often happens on light or calcareous soils. The leaves may show yellow stripes between the green veins. How do I fix the pH in my paddy field if it's too alkaline?

If your paddy field is too alkaline, you can use gypsum to help fix the problem. Apply 200-400 kg of gypsum per acre based on your soil test. Gypsum replaces sodium in the soil with calcium, which helps lower the pH. Spread the gypsum evenly and mix it into the soil before transplanting paddy. Adding farmyard manure and growing green manure crops like dhaincha can also help balance the pH. Keep the field flooded with 5-7 cm of water to further reduce alkalinity. Make sure to test your soil every 2-3 years to keep the pH in the right range.

What can I do to stop water from draining too fast in my paddy field?

Puddling the field after ploughing helps make a hard layer that holds water better. Make sure the field is level and fix the bunds. How do I manage the nutrients in my soil for better wheat growth?

Soil testing is key for managing nutrients in wheat. It tells you the levels of nitrogen, phosphorus, potassium, and other elements, so you can apply the right amount of fertilizer. If your soil has low organic carbon, you need more urea, and if it's high, you can use less. For phosphorus, you add more if the soil is low, and less if it's high. If you don't test, use the standard PAU recommendation for medium soil: 50 kg nitrogen, 25 kg P₂O₅, and potassium if needed. Apply all phosphorus and potassium at sowing, and split nitrogen into two doses. If irrigation is late, use urea sprays instead. Also, check for micronutrients like zinc and sulfur, and use tools like PAU-LCC to apply nitrogen only when needed. Using farmyard manure and biofertilizers can also help keep the soil healthy.

I'm growing wheat and the leaves are turning yellow. How much zinc should I spray?

If your wheat leaves are turning yellow, it might be a sign of zinc deficiency. In Uttar Pradesh, especially in areas like Ballia, zinc deficiency is common. If you see yellow patches between the leaf veins, you can spray 2 kg zinc sulphate and 6 kg urea in 320 liters of water per acre. If you've already used urea, mix the zinc sulphate with lime water instead. Make sure to use either urea or lime water with the zinc. This helps improve the crop's health and yield.

What can I do to stop pyrilla from hurting my maize?

To stop pyrilla from hurting your maize, keep the field clean by removing crop residues and nearby sugarcane plants. Sow early in mid-June to avoid peak pest time. Don't use too much nitrogen fertilizer, as it attracts pyrilla. Grow flowering plants like sunflowers, buckwheat, or dill to attract helpful insects that eat pyrilla. Check your plants regularly for nymphs and adults. If needed, use pesticides carefully. Early action is important to avoid damage.
What happens if pyrilla infestation is left untreated?
If left untreated, pyrilla can cause significant damage to maize plants, leading to reduced yield and quality.

My maize plants have leaves that look pale and not green, what's causing this?

This pale or white color on the youngest leaves is called white bud disease. It's caused by certain fungi like Pythium and Sclerospora. The leaves might look bleached, twisted, or not open properly. It happens more in wet, humid weather. To manage it, make sure the field drains well, use healthy seeds, and apply zinc if needed. You can also spray Metalaxyl or Fosetyl-Al as directed. Avoid overusing nitrogen and remove infected plants right away.
How should I apply the fungicides for white bud disease?
Spray Metalaxyl or Fosetyl-Al as directed, ensuring even coverage on the affected leaves.

My maize plants are growing slowly and the older leaves are turning pink, what can I do?

Your maize plants might be lacking phosphorus. This can cause slow growth and pinkish color on older leaves. To fix this, you should test the soil first. Phosphorus works best in soil with a pH of 6.5 to 7.5. If the soil is too alkaline, you may need to add gypsum. You can also apply DAP as a base fertilizer. For example, use 55 kg of DAP per acre for certain varieties. If the problem appears later, a 2% DAP foliar spray can help. Always follow the recommended amounts for your specific crop type.

Can I mix Imidacloprid and SAAF in the same spray for paddy?

What if I plant Raya late in November? Will it still grow well?

If you plant Raya late in November, it might face problems like aphids and frost. But there are special varieties made for late sowing. PBR 91 is a good one. It grows well even when planted late and gives about 8.1 quintals per acre. It’s also resistant to some pests. Another option is PBR 97, which matures faster and handles heat better. Don’t plant main-season varieties like RLC 3 or PBR 357 in November, or they’ll have a bad yield. Some other varieties like PHR 127 and RCH 1 are good for regular planting but not for late sowing.

My maize plants are getting white leaves, what could be the problem?

The white leaves on your maize plants could be due to white bud disease. It's often caused by Pythium or Sclerospora and happens when the field is too wet or humid. The youngest leaves might look pale or bleached. To manage it, make sure the soil drains well, use healthy seeds, and apply zinc. You can also spray Metalaxyl or Fosetyl-Al if the problem starts. Avoid overusing nitrogen and keep the field clean by removing sick plants.
How to manage irrigation to prevent white bud disease?
Ensure the soil drains well to avoid excessive moisture, which can contribute to white bud disease.

My citrus trees in Punjab are looking sick, what could be wrong and how do I fix it?

How can I use biochar in my paddy field, and what are the good things about it?

Biochar helps improve soil health and water retention for paddy. It makes the soil better at holding nutrients like nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium. It also helps microbes in the soil and makes roots grow better. To use it, mix 2 tonnes of biochar with 5-6 tonnes of farmyard manure. Spread it during field prep, 2-3 weeks before planting. Make sure to mix it well into the top 15 cm of soil. Wear a mask when handling it and keep it dry. Using it every 2-3 years can help keep the soil healthy and reduce the need for extra fertilizer.

My field is getting too wet, what can I do?

Planting trees around the field can lower the water level over time. Don't use heavy machines when the soil is wet, and plow deep to help water move better. If it rains a lot suddenly, try to remove the water fast with pumps or by making small cuts to let it flow out.

Don't use heavy machines when the soil is wet, as this can damage the soil structure and worsen waterlogging.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid waterlogging in the future?

My wheat field is getting attacked by birds, what can I do?

Birds like parrots, pigeons, and crows can damage wheat, especially when the grains are soft. You can try using scarecrows with bright materials, beat drums, or use noise-making devices. Fly kites that look like hawks or use balloons with eyes painted on them. Cover small areas with nets during the grain-filling stage. Also, watch the field in the early morning and late evening when birds are most active. Don't use poisons or harmful chemicals. Try using a mix of methods for better results.

To help your animals recover from foot-and-mouth disease, keep them isolated and in clean, dry areas. Clean their hooves regularly with solutions like copper sulfate or zinc sulfate. What can I do to fix waterlogged fields quickly?

If your fields get waterlogged, you should drain the water right away using temporary cuts or a pump. Avoid watering until the field dries. For long-term help, create surface drains or ditches before the season starts. Use raised beds or ridge-furrow systems to keep the root zone above water. Install mole or tile drains in heavy clay soils. Plant deep-rooted trees along the edges to lower the water table. Keep the soil healthy by avoiding heavy machines when it's wet and plough deeply to break hardpans.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid waterlogging in future seasons?

For long-term help, create surface drains or ditches before the season starts. Use raised beds or ridge-furrow systems to keep the root zone above water. Install mole or tile drains in heavy clay soils. Plant deep-rooted trees along the edges to lower the water table.

My cotton plants are getting damaged, and I think it's a type of weevil. What should I do?

It sounds like you might be dealing with stem or shoot weevils. Stem weevils make small holes in the stems and lay eggs. The grubs damage stems, branches, and roots, causing swelling and wilting. Shoot weevils make galls on the tips of shoots. To control them, plow deeply in summer, keep the field clean, rotate crops, and use trap crops like castor or marigold. You can also apply farmyard manure or neem cake. If you're not sure, take samples to the nearest KVK for help.

Then apply half the remaining nitrogen at the CRI stage, 20-25 days after sowing, using 100-120 kg urea. Apply the rest of the nitrogen at the tillering to jointing stage with 65-80 kg urea. Make sure the soil is moist when you apply it. Also, add 10-12 tonnes of well-decomposed farmyard manure before sowing.

The worms you're seeing are likely fruit borers, not spotted bollworms. They bore into the chilli pods and eat the inside. You can use neem cake at transplanting time to help. Apply 200 ml of Propiconazole or 300 g of Tebuconazole per acre with 200 liters of water. It's best to spray when the disease first appears or during the tillering to boot leaf stage. What can I do if my wheat crop is getting sick with rust and other problems?

If your wheat crop is getting sick with rust or other diseases, you can spray fungicides like Mancozeb, Propiconazole, or Tebuconazole. Apply them at the right time, like when the disease first shows up or during the tillering stage. For powdery mildew, sulfur works well. You can also use biological options like Trichoderma or Pseudomonas. Make sure to plant resistant varieties and keep the field clean by removing old plants. Always wear protective gear when applying chemicals and follow the instructions carefully.

You can use fungicides like Mancozeb 75% WP, Propiconazole 25% EC, or Tebuconazole 25% WG. For example, mix 800 grams of Mancozeb in 200-250 liters of water and spray. How much sulphur should I use for my cotton crop?

For hybrid or Bt cotton, you should use 150 kg of SSP per acre. For American cotton, 75 kg of SSP per acre is enough. SSP has 11–12% sulphur, so this gives around 16–18 kg of sulphur for hybrid cotton and 8–9 kg for American cotton. Sulphur helps with growth and prevents diseases like grey mildew and mites. For mites, you can spray 5 grams of water-soluble sulphur in a liter of water. Make sure to follow the local guidelines for best results.
What happens if I don't apply sulphur to my cotton crop?

To manage this, plant resistant varieties like Punjab Basmati 7, 5, or Pusa Basmati 1847. Use healthy seeds and avoid planting near wheat straw. Don't use too much nitrogen, and don't keep water standing in the field all the time. You can also spray copper hydroxide or copper sulphate to control the disease.

What can I do to fix the dwarf plants in my paddy field?

My mustard plants are getting lots of aphids, what can I do?

Aphids are a big problem for mustard, especially in cold and cloudy weather. They suck the sap from leaves, buds, and pods, making the plants small and the seeds not grow well. You can use yellow sticky traps to monitor them. If there are 50-60 aphids on a 10 cm part of the plant or 40-50% of the plants are infested, you should spray. Use insecticides like thiamethoxam, oxydemeton-methyl, dimethoate, or chlorpyrifos. Spray in the early morning or late evening. Also, plant early and use resistant varieties to help prevent the problem.
What happens if I don't treat aphids in my mustard crop?
If aphids are not treated, they suck the sap from leaves, buds, and pods, making the plants small and the seeds not grow well.
When should I spray the insecticides?
Spray in the early morning or late evening.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid aphids in mustard?
Plant early and use resistant varieties to help prevent the problem.

The tiny bugs are aphids, which suck the sap from your mustard plants. They can make the plants weak, cause leaves to curl, and stop the seeds from developing. To control them, try sowing early, use resistant varieties, and set up yellow sticky traps. If the aphid count gets high, spray insecticides like thiamethoxam or dimethoate. Make sure to check your plants regularly and spray when needed. Always follow the recommended doses and rotate insecticides to avoid resistance.

Check 12-16 plants every week starting in January. Spray if there are 50-60 aphids on the central shoot or if 0.5 to 1 cm of the shoot is covered. Also spray if 40-50% of the plants have aphids. You might notice white, cottony growth on the soil near wilted plants, especially in January or February. The stems might rot at the base. My paddy seedlings are getting too much water in the nursery, is that bad?

My wheat crop is getting damaged by some worms, what can I do?

My wheat plants are showing some yellow patches on the leaves, and I'm worried it's affecting the grains. What can I do?

It sounds like your wheat might have a boron deficiency. This happens in certain soils and can cause yellow or orange patches on the leaves. When should I plant wheat in Banda district, UP?

My paddy plants have yellow leaves, what's causing it and how do I fix it?

What can I do to stop dead heart in my paddy field?

To stop dead heart in your paddy, you can spray Fipronil 5% SC at 400-600 ml per acre or Chlorantraniliprole 18.5% SC at 60 ml per acre. You can also use Cartap Hydrochloride 4G granules at 7.5 kg per acre or Fipronil 0.3 G at 7.5 kg per acre mixed with sand. Also, remove egg masses, avoid too much nitrogen, keep water levels right, and use light traps or pheromone traps to control the pests.

My berseem crop is getting white stuff on the leaves and stems, what's going on?

The white stuff on your berseem crop is from a disease called stem rot. It happens in January and February when it's cold. The fungus in the soil attacks the bottom of the stems and makes them rot. My maize crop is getting yellow mold on the ears when it's almost ready to harvest. What's causing this?

The yellow mold on your maize is likely Aspergillus rot. It looks like an olive-green mold on the kernels and can spread like dust when you pull back the husk. This fungus makes a harmful toxin called aflatoxin, which is bad for animals and people. It happens more when the plants are stressed, like during hot, dry weather. Insects or birds can also hurt the ears and let the fungus in. To help, dry the maize to below 14% moisture and avoid damaging the kernels. Make sure the crop gets enough nutrients.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid Aspergillus rot in future crops?

To prevent Aspergillus rot in future crops, ensure the maize is dried to below 14% moisture after harvest, avoid damaging the kernels during handling, and provide the crop with adequate nutrients. Also, manage stress factors like drought and protect the ears from insects or birds.
What are the risks if Aspergillus rot is not addressed?

If left untreated, Aspergillus rot produces harmful aflatoxins that can contaminate the maize. These toxins are dangerous for both animals and humans, causing health issues when consumed. The mold also spreads through dust when husks are pulled back, worsening crop loss.
How can proper irrigation help prevent Aspergillus rot?

Proper irrigation helps prevent Aspergillus rot by reducing plant stress caused by hot, dry weather. Ensuring adequate water supply during critical growth stages minimizes the conditions favorable for the fungus to develop.

My wheat plants have a smut problem, how do I handle it?

Smut in wheat is caused by a fungus called Ustilago tritici. It replaces the grain with black spores. The best way is to treat the seeds before planting. You can use Carboxin 75 WP at 2.5 gm per kg of seed or Carbendazim 50 WP at the same rate. You can also use Tebuconazole 2 DS at 1.25 gm per kg. Another option is to mix Carboxin with Trichoderma viride. This helps the plants grow better. Treat the seeds one or two days before planting. If using Trichoderma, treat the seeds 72 hours before planting, then apply the fungicide 24 hours before planting.
How long should I wait after treating the seeds before planting?
Treat the seeds one or two days before planting. If using Trichoderma, treat the seeds 72 hours before planting, then apply the fungicide 24 hours before planting.
How many times should I apply the fungicide to the seeds?
The seeds should be treated once before planting, as specified in the answer.

What can I do to stop my potato crop from getting destroyed by late blight?

To stop late blight in potatoes, use healthy seed tubers and avoid leaving infected ones in the open. Bury them instead. Plant short-duration varieties like Kufri Chipsona-1 or Kufri Pushkar. Spray the crop with Propineb, Mancozeb, or Chlorothalonil before symptoms appear. Apply the spray in the first week of November. Also, use high ridge culture to keep the tubers dry. If the weather is cool and humid, the disease can spread fast. Make sure to follow these steps early to prevent losses.

How do I make Panchagavya for my paddy field?

To make Panchagavya, take 7 kg of cow dung and 1 kg of ghee. Mix them well in a pot for 3 days, morning and evening. After that, add 10 liters of cow urine and 10 liters of water. Keep stirring twice a day. Let it ferment for 15 to 30 days. You can also add some jaggery, banana, or coconut water to help the microbes grow. Once ready, filter it and use it for your paddy. You can spray it on the leaves or use it as a seed treatment.

How do cotton bolls open, and what causes them to do that?

Cotton bolls open because of a hormone called ethylene. When the boll is young, another hormone called auxin stops it from opening too soon. As the boll gets mature, auxin goes down and ethylene goes up. This makes the boll start to crack open. The boll has a weak line inside, and when it dries, the outer covering splits. But if the boll isn't healthy, or if there's not enough water, it might not open right. Good care, like proper water and nutrients, helps bolls open properly.

How deep should I plant the maize seeds for good fodder growth?

You should plant the maize seeds about 3 to 5 cm deep. This helps the seeds germinate well and develop strong roots. Use a planter or a seed-cum-fertilizer drill for best results. Keep the rows 30 cm apart and use around 30 kg of seeds per acre. You can plant from March to September, but it's better to plant between April and August to avoid pests. You can grow up to three fodder crops a year. You can also plant using traditional methods or no-till farming.

What is Pusa Decomposer and how do I use it for my paddy field?

Pusa Decomposer is a microbial mix that helps break down paddy straw into compost, which is good for your soil. It’s made by a research institute in Delhi and works fast. You can use it in capsule or liquid form. To make the solution, mix jaggery and besan in water, add the capsules, and let it sit for ten days. Once ready, spray it over your field after harvesting. This helps the straw break down quicker, so you can plant again faster. Use 5 liters per 10 quintals of straw or 10 liters per acre.

Use 10 liters of Pusa Decomposer solution per acre.

How can I use the Pusa decomposer to break down paddy straw in my field?

To use Pusa decomposer, mix four capsules with 5 liters of water, add 150 grams of jaggery and 50 grams of besan, then let it sit for 10 days. Once ready, spray the solution over the paddy straw in your field. After spraying, mix the straw into the soil and water the field. This helps break down the straw faster and improves soil health.

My wheat plants are getting damaged by insects, what can I do?

Termites can harm your wheat after sowing and near maturity. To stop them, plough deeply in summer, use well-rotted manure, and avoid sowing too late. You can treat seeds with Chlorpyriphos 20% EC or Thiamethoxam 70% WS. Neem cake also helps. If termites are bad, use Fipronil 0.30% GR or Chlorpyriphos 20% EC. Aphids suck sap, so use yellow traps and bird perches. If they get too bad, spray Imidacloprid, Thiamethoxam, or Quinalphos. Pink stem borer damages young plants, so keep irrigation regular and spray Quinalphos when you see symptoms.
How often should I spray Quinalphos for pink stem borer?
Spray Quinalphos when you see symptoms of pink stem borer damage on young plants.
What preventive measures can I take for pink stem borer?
Keep irrigation regular and spray Quinalphos when you see symptoms of pink stem borer damage on young plants.

What pests and diseases harm my wheat crop and how can I manage them?

Your wheat can be affected by pests like aphids, armyworms, termites, and weevils, as well as diseases like rusts, powdery mildew, and Karnal bunt. For rust, you can use Propiconazole or Tebuconazole. To control termites, apply neem cake or treat seeds with Thiamethoxam. For aphids, use Quinalphos or Thiamethoxam. Cultural methods like proper spacing, crop rotation, and avoiding too much nitrogen help too. You can also use sticky traps for aphids and burn stubble after harvest to reduce disease spread.
Kitna dosa Propiconazole ka lagana chahiye rust control ke liye?

What can I do to stop my wheat crops from getting damaged by pests and diseases?

Wheat can be attacked by pests like aphids, termites, and armyworms, and diseases like rust and karnal bunt. To protect your crop, use resistant varieties like HD 2501 or MACS 2496. Apply proper farming practices like proper land leveling, good drainage, and avoid overusing nitrogen. For chemical control, spray Propiconazole or Thiram as needed. For termites, you can use neem cake or treat seeds with Thiamethoxam. For aphids, use Quinalphos or Thiamethoxam. Regular field checks and using natural predators can also help control pests.

My onion plants have tiny bugs on the leaves and some are turning yellow. What's going on and how do I fix it?

The tiny bugs on your onion leaves could be mites, like red spider mites or eriophyid mites. They feed on the leaves and cause yellowing, stippling, and webbing. You can spray the plants with water to wash them off. Also, encourage natural predators like ladybugs and lacewings. Avoid planting onions in the same spot every year and keep the soil clean. Some plants like marigolds can help too.

Some diseases, like karnal bunt, are hard to spot in the field but can be seen after harvest. To manage this, use proper farming methods like good drainage, crop rotation, and resistant varieties. You can also spray chemicals like propiconazole or thiram. For pests, use neem cake or chemical treatments like chlorpyrifos. Keeping your field clean and checking regularly helps too.

What should I do to make sure my fodder maize gets enough phosphorus and potassium?

To ensure your fodder maize gets enough phosphorus and potassium, use well-decomposed farmyard manure at 10 tonnes per acre before planting. This helps supply most of the phosphorus and potassium needed. You can also apply chemical fertilizers like SSP or DAP along with MOP and urea. For sandy loam to clay loam soils, use 100 kg SSP or 35 kg DAP, 15 kg MOP, and 70 kg urea per acre. For loamy sand to sandy soils, use 50 kg SSP or 18 kg DAP, 8 kg MOP, and 35 kg urea. If using DAP, reduce urea by 15 kg or 8 kg depending on the amount. Drill half the nitrogen and all the phosphorus and potassium at sowing. Apply the rest of the nitrogen after 3-4 weeks. Always check your soil test report before applying fertilizers to avoid overuse and ensure better yield.
When should I apply the remaining nitrogen after sowing?
Apply the rest of the nitrogen after 3-4 weeks. Always check your soil test report before applying fertilizers to avoid overuse and ensure better yield.
How should I apply the fertilizers during sowing and after?

Drill half the nitrogen and all the phosphorus and potassium at sowing. Apply the rest of the nitrogen after 3-4 weeks. Always check your soil test report before applying fertilizers to avoid overuse and ensure better yield.
What is the dosage of SSP or DAP for sandy loam soils?
For sandy loam to clay loam soils, use 100 kg SSP or 35 kg DAP, 15 kg MOP, and 70 kg urea per acre. If using DAP, reduce urea by 15 kg depending on the amount.

My onions are getting messed up by tiny bugs. What are they and how do I get rid of them?

The tiny bugs on your onions are mites. There are three main types: red spider mites, eriophyid mites, and bulb mites. Red spider mites eat the leaves and make them look speckled. They also make webs. Eriophyid mites damage the leaves and can spread viruses. Bulb mites get inside the bulbs and rot them. To control them, spray the plants with water. Also, encourage natural predators like ladybugs and lacewings. Don’t plant onions in the same place every year. Use crops like marigold or buckwheat nearby to help keep mites away.

My maize plants are not growing well, and the leaves are turning purple and yellow. What can I do?

Your maize might be lacking phosphorus and potassium. Phosphorus deficiency causes purple leaves and poor growth. Potassium deficiency makes leaf edges yellow and brown. To fix this, add 10 tonnes of farmyard manure per acre before planting. You can also use fertilizers like SSP or DAP with MOP and urea. For sandy soils, use less fertilizer. Always check your soil test report before applying nutrients. This helps improve the quality and yield of your fodder.
How much fertilizer should I apply per acre?

The exact dosage for fertilizers like SSP, DAP, MOP, and urea should be determined based on your soil test report. Always follow the recommendations provided in the report to ensure proper nutrient application.
How should I apply the fertilizers?
Follow the recommendations in your soil test report for the correct method of application.
Kab tak fertilizers apply karna chahiye?
Fertilizer application timing should follow recommendations in your soil test report. This ensures nutrients are available during critical growth stages of your maize.

What are the common problems in my wheat crop and how can I fix them?

Wheat can get hurt by pests like aphids, armyworms, and termites, and diseases like rust and Karnal bunt. Karnal bunt is hard to spot early but shows up after harvest. It spreads through infected seeds. To manage it, use clean seeds, avoid too much water, and spray Propiconazole. For rust, use resistant seeds and spray chemicals like Propiconazole or Tebuconazole. Termites can damage roots, so use neem cake or spray Chlorpyrifos. Aphids suck sap, so use sticky traps or spray Quinalphos. Keep your field clean and use proper farming methods to stay ahead of these issues.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid Karnal bunt in my wheat crop?
Use clean seeds and avoid too much water to prevent Karnal bunt in your wheat crop.
What are the symptoms of Karnal bunt in wheat?
Karnal bunt is hard to spot early but shows up after harvest.
Which chemicals are used to control aphids in wheat?
Spray Quinalphos to control aphids in your wheat crop.

My onions have some tiny bugs on the leaves, what can I do about it?

The tiny bugs on your onion leaves are probably mites. They feed on the leaves and can cause yellow or discolored spots. You can wash them off with water. Also, try to keep the area clean and avoid planting onions in the same spot too often. Encourage natural enemies like ladybugs and lacewings to help control the mites.
What happens if I don't treat the mites?
If left untreated, the mites will continue feeding on the leaves, causing more yellow or discolored spots, which can affect the onion's growth and yield.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid mites in the future?
To prevent mites, keep the area clean and avoid planting onions in the same spot too often. This reduces the chances of mite infestation in future crops.

You can wash them off with water.

My onions are getting messed up by tiny bugs, what can I do?

Onions can get attacked by three types of mites. Red spider mites cause yellow spots on leaves and make them fall off. Eriophyid mites damage the leaves and bulbs, causing them to twist and turn. Bulb mites rot the bulbs and stop them from growing. To manage them, spray the plants with water to wash off the mites. Also, encourage natural enemies like ladybugs, lacewings, and predatory mites. Avoid planting onions after other crops like garlic or cole crops. Use flood irrigation to reduce mite levels in the soil.
What happens if I don't manage the mites on my onions?
If left untreated, bulb mites can rot the bulbs and prevent them from growing, while other mites can damage leaves and bulbs, leading to reduced yield or crop failure.
What irrigation method is recommended for mite control in onions?
Flood irrigation is recommended to reduce mite levels in the soil.

What is waste decomposer and how can it help me with my paddy field?

Waste decomposer is a special mix of microbes that helps break down paddy straw into useful compost. It’s made by a place in New Delhi and helps farmers use less chemical fertilizer. What are the best ways to stop my wheat from getting rust?

To stop rust in wheat, use resistant varieties like HD 2501 or MACS 2496. Avoid too much nitrogen, and spray Propiconazole 25% EC or Tebuconazole 25% WG. Also, rotate crops and don't plant wheat in the same field every year. Keep an eye on the weather, as rust likes cool and humid conditions.
